reason: Continuing appropriations vehicle with DHS authority—classic smokescreen vector for buried riders, exemptions, and fiscal redirects. Title alone signals high-risk appropriations mechanics (CR language, discretionary spending authority, DHS scope). Kernel audit critical.
vectors: Defense/DHS smokescreen: CR mechanics enable buried policy riders and exemptions
Fiscal bleed: Continuing appropriations often contain debt-limit workarounds or TGA redirects
Regulatory capture: DHS scope permits broad carve-outs for federal contractors or border-tech vendors
Missing summary: Absence of provided details increases exploit surface—flag for full text audit
title: Homeland Security and Further Additional Continuing Appropriations Act, 2026.
